Power BIDAXMySQLStar SchemaPower Query
Business Insight 360
Business Insight 360 is a full-scale Power BI business intelligence platform built for AtliQ Hardware — a global PC hardware company operating across APAC, EU, North America and Latin America. The project covers the complete data pipeline: raw data extracted from a MySQL data warehouse using SQL stored procedures, transformed in Power Query, modelled as a star schema with 2 fact tables and 8 dimension tables, and analysed using 50+ DAX measures. The platform delivers 5 interactive dashboard views — Finance (dynamic P&L benchmarking), Sales (scatter matrix, customer toggle), Marketing (bookmark toggles, conditional formatting), Supply Chain (forecast accuracy, EI/OOS risk classification) and Executive (KPI sparklines, market share trend). Key findings include a $522M operational expense gap, a 45.67% gross margin premium channel opportunity and critical out-of-stock risk in Peripherals and Accessories. Published live on Power BI Service.
⚙️ GitHub 💼 LinkedIn Post ▶ YouTube 🔴 Live Dashboard
PythonPandasMatplotlibSeabornNumPy
Hospitality Performance Analytics — Python EDA
This Python EDA project analyses 134,590 hotel booking records across 25 properties in 4 Indian cities to identify why a 20-year hotel group is losing revenue and market share to competitors. Using Pandas, Matplotlib and Seaborn, the analysis covers the full analytical workflow — data validation, outlier removal using Mean ± 3×Std, feature engineering (Occupancy %, RevPAR, Cancellation Rate), and 8 structured business insights. Key findings include a 22-point weekday occupancy gap signalling missed corporate traveller demand, a Delhi pricing paradox where the highest occupancy generates the lowest revenue, and a systemic 25% cancellation rate across all booking platforms. The project concludes with 6 prioritised recommendations and a total addressable revenue opportunity of ₹410–480M, demonstrating end-to-end analytical thinking from raw data to boardroom-ready decisions.
⚙️ GitHub 💼 LinkedIn Post ▶ YouTube
SQLMySQLPower BIExcel
SQL Ad-Hoc Business Intelligence Analysis
Executive ad-hoc insights project for AtliQ Hardware (FY2020–FY2021). The goal was to convert 10 leadership ad-hoc business questions into decision-grade insights. I wrote SQL queries, validated outputs, and consolidated results into an executive dashboard and a management presentation. Key findings include: portfolio growth of 36.33% (245→334 products) with uneven segment expansion, discount governance risk with top India customers averaging ~29–31% discounts, strong seasonality (Q1 33.72% vs Q3 9.99% of annual volume), and channel dependency (Retailer 73.21% of gross sales). The final deliverables include SQL scripts, Excel/CSV outputs, dashboard snapshots, and an executive roadmap with actions and KPIs for leadership tracking.
⚙️ GitHub 💼 LinkedIn Post ▶ YouTube
ExcelPower PivotDAXPower QueryFinancial Modelling
Sales & Finance Analytics
Transformed AtliQ Hardware's fragmented Excel reporting into a unified BI system, cutting monthly reporting time by 83% (12 hrs → 2 hrs) and preventing $180K+ in annual margin leakage through automated discount governance. Built a star-schema data model using Power Query, Power Pivot, and DAX, connecting Sales, Finance, and Marketing under a Single Source of Truth. Delivered executive-standard P&L reporting (monthly, quarterly, yearly), Target vs. Actual tracking, and a Discount Scenario Tool with built-in approval tiers (10%/20% thresholds). Beyond the technical build, I led the full BA lifecycle — stakeholder interviews, Mini-BRD, RACI matrix, KPI Dictionary, and UAT sign-off, ensuring every metric was fully trusted at C-Suite level. The result: zero reconciliation issues, 83% faster discount approvals, and 100% report adoption across all deals above the 10% threshold.
⚙️ GitHub 💼 LinkedIn Post ▶ YouTube
